mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate over the prior three months reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, which mechanically increases present equity values and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are negative in more than 50 percent of observations during sustained FEDFUNDS declines, or the Rank IC is below 0.02 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 9.5 percent, it signals severe labor market slack that depresses aggregate income and consumption expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next sixty trading days as earnings revisions turn sharply negative.
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts positive 60-day returns in over 55 percent of instances when UNRATE exceeds 9.5 percent, or the signal Rank IC is positive over a 2-year walk-forward period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and industrial production indicates a broad-based demand expansion that is matched by supply capacity, reducing margin pressure and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive momentum in RSAFS and INDPRO fails to produce positive S&P 500 20-day returns in at least 55 percent of occurrences, or the interaction term Rank IC falls below 0.03 over a rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: During a zero-interest-rate policy regime, month-over-month increases in consumer prices signal reflation and nominal revenue growth without offsetting monetary tightening, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next sixty trading days.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS is below 0.5 percent, month-over-month CPIAUCSL increases coincide with negative S&P 500 60-day returns in more than half of observed months, or the conditional Rank IC is below 0.02 over any 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increase in industrial production signals improving corporate operating leverage and capacity utilization, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days as the information is incorporated into equity prices.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month changes in INDPRO coincide with negative S&P 500 5-day returns in more than 50 percent of instances, or the Rank IC is not significantly positive (p > 0.05) over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts below the 600 thousand unit threshold signal weak construction activity and deteriorating household formation, which drags durable goods demand and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: When HOUST is below 600 thousand, S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 55 percent of months, or the binary signal accuracy is below 50 percent over a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month payroll employment growth signals expanding household income and aggregate demand, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next sixty trading days as earnings expectations are revised upward.
failure_condition: Month-over-month increases in PAYEMS coincide with negative S&P 500 60-day returns in more than 50 percent of observations, or the Rank IC is below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month consumer price inflation exceeding a 3 percent annualized rate signals binding supply constraints that force the Federal Reserve to tighten financial conditions, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL above 3 percent annualized coincides with positive S&P 500 5-day returns in more than 55 percent of triggered instances, or the signal Rank IC is positive over a 2-year walk-forward window.
